title: Isolepis perpusilla
intro: Isolepis perpusilla, also known as the Dwarf club-rush, is a small, grass-like perennial plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Isolepis perpusilla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Isolepis perpusilla is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of teas, tinctures, and ointments to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Isolepis perpusilla belongs to the family Cyperaceae. The genus Isolepis is a group of sedges. The species perpusilla is a specific type within the genus Isolepis. This classification suggests that Isolepis perpusilla is a type of sedge plant. It is a part of the larger kingdom Plantae.
